machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (bitopic; links the IM platform to the secretin OutD)
machinery_genes.quote: The outer membrane pore formed by the secretin OutD and the inner membrane protein OutC are two key components of the secretion complex, involved in secretion specificity.
machinery_genes.note: Login et al 2010, E. chrysanthemi (= D. dadantii) Out T2SS. OutC = GspC-family IM platform protein, first gene of the named outC-S operon. Bitopic IM protein with a periplasmic PDZ/HR domain (Bouley 2001, PMID 11327762).

machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin (forms the OM channel/pore for substrate exit)
machinery_genes.quote: OutD is an outer membrane component of the main terminal branch of the general secretory pathway (GSP) in Erwinia chrysanthemi.
machinery_genes.note: Shevchik, Robert-Baudouy & Condemine 1997 (EMBO J). OutD forms outer-membrane multimers ('OutD could form a channel-like structure in the outer membrane') = the GspD secretin. Erwinia chrysanthemi 3937 is the strain now reclassified as Dickeya dadantii 3937.

machinery_genes.role: cytoplasmic secretion ATPase (energizes the apparatus; Walker-motif ATP-binding component)
machinery_genes.quote: we investigated interaction between OutE, the putative ATP binding component, and OutL, an inner membrane protein.
machinery_genes.note: Py, Loiseau & Barras 1999, E. chrysanthemi Out T2SS. OutE = GspE secretion ATPase; the paper shows an intact Walker A motif in OutE is required for secretion and that OutE uses ATP to control assembly of the type II machinery.

machinery_genes.quote: Previously, we reported the key role of the OutF protein in forming, along with OutELM, an inner membrane platform in the Out T2S system.
machinery_genes.note: Douet, Expert, Barras & Py 2008, E. chrysanthemi (= D. dadantii). OutF = GspF-family integral IM platform protein; one of the four OutEFLM inner-membrane platform components.

machinery_genes.role: major pseudopilin (builds the bulk of the pseudopilus piston)
machinery_genes.quote: In D. dadantii, the major pseudopilin or the major subunit of the pseudopilus is called OutG.
machinery_genes.note: Jacobsen et al 2022 (Biomol NMR Assign), explicitly on the Dickeya dadantii Out T2SS. OutG = GspG major pseudopilin, processed by a prepilin peptidase and anchored in the IM.

machinery_genes.quote: the major pseudopilin G composing the bulk of the pseudopilus, and the minor pseudopilins H, I, J and K that form a complex initiating the pseudopilus formation
machinery_genes.note: Jacobsen et al 2022, D. dadantii Out T2SS. OutH = GspH minor pseudopilin. (The four out genes outH-outK were the first Out genes sequenced, as pulH-pulK homologs: He et al 1991, PMID 1992458.)

machinery_genes.quote: the major pseudopilin G composing the bulk of the pseudopilus, and the minor pseudopilins H, I, J and K that form a complex initiating the pseudopilus formation
machinery_genes.note: Jacobsen et al 2022, D. dadantii. OutI = GspI minor pseudopilin. Independently shown to be periplasmic and to interact with OutJ and OutD/OutL (Douet 2004, PMID 14990257).

machinery_genes.quote: Results obtained using this approach suggested that OutJ (a pseudopilin) was able to interact with (i) OutD, the outer membrane secretin, (ii) OutI, mainly located in the periplasm, and (iii) OutL, an inner membrane protein.
machinery_genes.note: Douet, Loiseau, Barras & Py 2004 (Res Microbiol), systematic two-hybrid of the E. chrysanthemi Out apparatus; OutJ = GspJ minor pseudopilin. This abstract also names OutD (secretin), OutI and OutL. The same minor-pseudopilin set is restated for D. dadantii in Jacobsen 2022 (PMID 35482172).

machinery_genes.quote: the major pseudopilin G composing the bulk of the pseudopilus, and the minor pseudopilins H, I, J and K that form a complex initiating the pseudopilus formation
machinery_genes.note: Jacobsen et al 2022, D. dadantii. OutK = GspK minor pseudopilin. outK was among the first four out genes sequenced as a pulK homolog (He et al 1991, PMID 1992458).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane platform component (binds the ATPase OutE)
machinery_genes.quote: we investigated interaction between OutE, the putative ATP binding component, and OutL, an inner membrane protein. We showed... that OutE and OutL interact directly.
machinery_genes.note: Py, Loiseau & Barras 1999, E. chrysanthemi. OutL = GspL IM platform protein that bridges the cytoplasmic ATPase OutE to the membrane; one of the OutEFLM platform components (Douet 2008, PMID 18978048).

machinery_genes.quote: the key role of the OutF protein in forming, along with OutELM, an inner membrane platform in the Out T2S system
machinery_genes.note: Douet et al 2008, E. chrysanthemi (= D. dadantii). OutM = GspM IM platform protein; named as the 'M' of the OutEFLM / OutELM inner-membrane platform. The paper repeatedly refers to the 'OutEFLM-forming platform'.

machinery_genes.role: prepilin peptidase (matures the pseudopilins OutG/H/I/J; bifunctional, often shared with type IV pilus)
machinery_genes.quote: The nucleotide sequence of eight additional out genes reveals homology with pulC through pulG, pulL, pulM, pulO, and other genes involved in secretion by various gram-negative bacteria... outO appears to be transcribed separately from outC through outM.
machinery_genes.note: Lindeberg & Collmer 1992 (J Bacteriol). outO is the pulO/GspO prepilin-peptidase homolog of the Out cluster, transcribed separately from the outC-outM operon (hence the operon label 'outC-S' spans through outO/outS). GspO is the pre-pilin peptidase that cleaves the OutG/H/I/J pseudopilin leader sequences. Quote is verbatim from the article abstract (the article full text is available only as scanned page images).

machinery_genes.role: pilotin (outer-membrane lipoprotein chaperone; targets/inserts and stabilizes the secretin OutD)
machinery_genes.quote: Proper insertion of the secretin OutD in the outer membrane requires the presence of OutS. OutS is an outer-membrane lipoprotein that interacts directly with OutD.
machinery_genes.note: Shevchik & Condemine 1998 (Microbiology). OutS = the GspS/PulS-family pilotin; it is the terminal gene of the named operon (outC-S). OutS is a secretin-specific accessory lipoprotein, not the secreted substrate. It functionally substitutes for K. oxytoca PulS (Hardie 1996, PMID 8971717).

notes: The 'Out' system is the archetypal plant-pathogen T2SS, secreting >15 plant-cell-wall-degrading enzymes (pectate lyases, pectin methylesterase, cellulase, etc.) in Dickeya dadantii 3937 (formerly Erwinia chrysanthemi 3937; the EC16 / 3937 lab strains are the same Out system). The strain name in the older apparatus papers is 'Erwinia chrysanthemi'; this is the direct senior synonym of Dickeya dadantii, so no cross-strain transfer is involved beyond the genus rename. Core apparatus fully documented gene-by-gene: secretin OutD; cytoplasmic ATPase OutE; inner-membrane platform OutC, OutF, OutL, OutM (the OutEFLM platform of Douet 2008); major pseudopilin OutG and minor pseudopilins OutH, OutI, OutJ, OutK; prepilin peptidase OutO; and the secretin pilotin OutS. The operon label 'outC-S' (outC through outS) matches: outC-outM form one transcribed operon, with outO and the pilotin outS encoded in the same locus but transcribed separately (Lindeberg & Collmer 1992). OutG and the H/I/J/K minor-pseudopilin assignment are confirmed in a Dickeya-dadantii-specific NMR paper (Jacobsen 2022). pulN has no Out homolog in this cluster (Lindeberg 1992: 'a pulN homolog is not present'), so no OutN is listed. OutB (PMID 10746767) is an accessory secretin-stabilizing protein associated with the Out machine but is NOT in the canonical Gsp core nomenclature and was deliberately excluded as non-core (and is outside the GspC-S brief vocabulary). Deliberately excluded as non-machinery: the secreted substrates (PelA-E pectate lyases, Cel5/EGZ cellulase, PemA pectin methylesterase, the curated effectors Dda3937_*), the Sec/Tat inner-membrane translocon (general export, not T2SS), and KdgR/regulatory factors. Lowest-confidence call: OutO, whose only literature quote here is the 1992 operon/homology abstract (full text is scanned images); its GspO prepilin-peptidase identity is well supported by homology to pulO and by the pseudopilin-processing role described for the system, but no single-gene functional-characterization quote was located.
